content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Both amendments identify real divergences in same-bead project handling. Amendment 1 correctly flags that reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation hard-codes ExplicitTaskSourceOnly (src/cli/project.rs:635) while find_existing_bead_project uses IncludeLegacyDefaultIdFallback (src/cli/project.rs:933) — a user passing --project-id for a bead whose only prior project is a legacy default-named failed/paused record with lineage evidence can mint a parallel project, or at best hit a DuplicateProject error without guidance. Amendment 2 correctly flags that active_same_bead_conflict_error maps Failed to RunStartFailed (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2834-2854) while Paused maps to DuplicateActiveBead, so find_existing_bead_project (src/cli/project.rs:886-903) returns a hard MilestoneOperationFailed instead of the project id — blocking controller recovery of resumable failed beads whenever active_task_id is missing, despite load_run_action mapping Failed→Resume. Both are genuine correctness defects worth landing.

## Votes

- `fr-14-5b3661e5`: Accept
  Verified: reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation calls matching_bead_project_statuses with SameBeadProjectMatchMode::ExplicitTaskSourceOnly at src/cli/project.rs:635, and matching_bead_project_ids returns early at src/cli/project.rs:805-807 before consulting lineage evidence. find_existing_bead_project uses IncludeLegacyDefaultIdFallback at src/cli/project.rs:933 and relies on legacy_default_project_has_lineage_evidence at src/cli/project.rs:813-822, 827-840. The divergence is real: when an operator passes --project-id=<custom> for a bead whose prior failed/paused attempt lives in a legacy default-named project with matching task-runs.ndjson evidence but no task_source, nothing in the create path catches it — reject_duplicate_active_bead_creation only inspects running lineage (src/adapters/fs.rs:5098), reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation misses legacy records, and project_exists won't fire because the new id differs. Even with default naming the operator gets a bare DuplicateProject without the resume guidance the explicit-task-source path produces. Aligning create-time validation with find_existing_bead_project's mode matches existing tests at src/cli/project.rs:3661-3684.
- `fr-14-2563e079`: Accept
  Verified: active_same_bead_conflict_error returns DuplicateActiveBead for Running (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2790-2811) and Paused (2812-2833) but RunStartFailed for Failed (2834-2855). find_existing_bead_project at src/cli/project.rs:886-903 maps DuplicateActiveBead to Ok(Some(project_id)) but funnels RunStartFailed through milestone_run_project_resolution_error (src/cli/project.rs:708-717) into MilestoneOperationFailed, which ensure_project_for_controller (src/cli/milestone.rs:1148-1159) cannot adopt. Meanwhile load_run_action at src/cli/milestone.rs:1247-1252 groups Paused | Failed | Running into MilestoneRunAction::Resume, so the controller is perfectly capable of handling a failed snapshot once it has the project id. The asymmetry between Paused (adoptable) and Failed (unrecoverable) is a real defect: if active_task_id is ever missing (new run, cleared state, fresh operator session), a normal failed-retry bead is stranded in needs_operator for no semantic reason. The fix must preserve the repair-path RunStartFailed signals produced by validate_snapshot_attempt_matches_lineage and active_lineage_repair_error, but the normal failed-retry case should map to Ok(Some(project_id)).
